user_query:
I help coordinate volunteers at Joint Base Andrews, and I want to lock down the logic that flags active volunteers before we share reports with the spouses club. In this workspace, there’s a small Python module that parses a roster and checks eligibility against rules in YAML. Please do the following:

1) Inspect the project layout under src/, config/, and data/ to understand how the code works. The core code is in src/eligibility.py, the roster data is at data/roster.csv, and the column mapping and rules are under config/fieldmap.yaml and config/eligibility.yaml.

2) Make the date-based checks deterministic by editing config/eligibility.yaml to add a fixed reference date: reference_date: "2022-04-01". Keep the existing values for min_hours and bg_check_valid_years. This change must be used by tests so they don’t depend on today’s date.

3) Write pytest tests in tests/test_eligibility.py that read the local CSV and YAML config files and validate both parsing and eligibility classification. Specifically:
   - Use parse_roster(data/roster.csv, config/fieldmap.yaml) to produce normalized records. Assert that there are exactly 5 records and that each record’s keys include exactly: name, email, unit, rank, hours_q1, bg_check_date.
   - Load rules from config/eligibility.yaml (including the reference_date you added) and classify each volunteer using the functions in src/eligibility.py. Assert the following outcomes by full name, based on the deterministic reference date 2022-04-01 and rules (min_hours=10, bg_check_valid_years=2):
     • Sgt. Alex Rivera — active (no reasons)
     • Jamie Lee — ineligible with reason set containing "low_hours"
     • Morgan Patel — ineligible with reason set containing both "missing_email" and "expired_bg_check"
     • Taylor Kim — ineligible with reason set containing "expired_bg_check"
     • Jordan Smith — ineligible with reason set containing "invalid_email"
   - Also assert that exactly one volunteer is active and it is "Sgt. Alex Rivera".

4) Run the tests and save a JUnit XML report to output/test_results.xml (for example, by running pytest with --junitxml output/test_results.xml). Ensure output/test_results.xml is created as an artifact.

Notes/constraints:
- Do not fetch anything from the Internet; work only with the files provided locally.
- The tests must exercise the real parsing and classification by reading data/roster.csv and the YAML files under config/.
- Only modify configuration files required for determinism (config/eligibility.yaml). Do not change src/eligibility.py unless you discover an obvious typo preventing the tests from importing or running.
- Deliverables: the modified config/eligibility.yaml, a new tests/test_eligibility.py, and the test report at output/test_results.xml. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
